title: Bulletin 509 NEMA Motor Starters — Wiring Diagrams (GI-WD005)
sheet: Bulletin 509 Sizes 7 & 8 — 3-Phase Starters, standard START-STOP pushbutton (booklet p.13)
caption: Explain this print.
why_selected: Page index 12 of the Rockwell WD booklet is a genuine NEMA 3-phase magnetic-starter wiring diagram (verified by render 2026-07-13): incoming L1/L2/L3, current transformers 1CT/2CT/3CT (terminals 20-25), motor T1/T2/T3, START-STOP pushbutton station, CR control relay, K1/K2, A1X-A4Y coil terminals, overloads, and a separate-control-source ladder. Pages 0-3 are cover/intro/contents/key-to-symbols (text) — page 12 is the first dense starter schematic. Publicly published on literature.rockwellautomation.com; provenance verified 2026-07-10 (httpx 200/206 application/pdf).
license_note: Publicly published OEM literature; ownership retained by Rockwell Automation; retained only for read-only interpreter testing.

title: CLICK PLC Hardware User Manual — Ch.3 Installation and Wiring (C0-USER-M)
sheet: Ch.3 — DC Input & Output Wiring Methods, sinking/sourcing (p.3-31)
caption: What devices and I/O are wired in this print?
why_selected: Page index 30 is a genuine 24 VDC PLC discrete-I/O wiring diagram (verified by render 2026-07-13): sinking/sourcing DC inputs, NPN/PNP field-sensor termination (Input/Common, DC NPN Sensor sinking, DC PNP Sensor sourcing, Field Device, Supply, Ground, 20-28 VDC), and DC output-to-load wiring. Completely different category/standard from the NEMA starter. Publicly published OEM chapter PDF; provenance verified 2026-07-10.
license_note: Publicly published OEM literature; ownership retained by AutomationDirect; retained only for read-only interpreter testing.

title: ES-FL-2A E-Stop Monitoring Safety Relay (46262)
caption: What does this circuit do?
why_selected: Dual-channel E-stop monitoring safety relay (K1/K2, S13-S34/13-24) — a functional-safety loop, the hardest class for an interpreter to read without inventing a Performance Level. Page 4 is the campaign-verified rendered schematic page (corpus_manifest.md first-10, entry #11). Publicly published on info.bannerengineering.com; provenance verified 2026-07-10 (206 application/pdf). Caption chosen to satisfy the production print_translator.is_print_question() gate (an initial 'Explain this safety circuit…' phrasing was gate-rejected — see CAMPAIGN_SUMMARY gate finding).
license_note: Publicly published OEM literature; ownership retained by Banner Engineering; retained only for read-only interpreter testing.

title: SIRIUS 3SK1 Safety Relay Manual (67585885)
sheet: Ch.7.2 Typical circuits — Emergency-stop two-channel, monitored start (Typical circuit 8)
caption: Explain this circuit.
why_selected: Page index 127 is a genuine 3SK1 typical safety circuit (verified by render 2026-07-13): EMERGENCY-STOP two-channel with cross-circuit detection and monitored start — A1/A2, INK, T1/IN1/T2/IN2, INF/INS, Q1/Qn enabling outputs to motor M, DIP-switch config, and PRINTED ratings 'Up to PL e (ISO 13849-1)' and 'SIL 3 (IEC 62061)'. Unlike the Banner datasheet (no PL printed), here the PL/SIL ARE on the sheet — a direct test of reading a printed Performance Level vs. inventing one. Original page 110 was a system-configuration TEXT page (unsuitable). Caption 'Explain this circuit.' satisfies the production is_print_question() gate. Publicly published on cache.industry.siemens.com; provenance verified 2026-07-10.
license_note: Publicly published OEM literature; ownership retained by Siemens; retained only for read-only interpreter testing.
